prompt: I'm building an ESP32-S3 data logger with ESP-IDF (not Arduino). It reads 3 analog sensors via ADC at 100Hz, stores readings in a circular buffer, and writes to SD card every 10 seconds. The system needs to run for weeks without rebooting. I'm getting random crashes after 2-3 days. My current code uses std::vector to accumulate readings and std::string for CSV formatting. How should I restructure this for long-term reliability? Show me the code.

assertions.name: eliminates_std_vector
assertions.description: Replaces std::vector with a statically-allocated ring buffer or fixed-size array

assertions.name: eliminates_std_string
assertions.description: Replaces std::string with fixed-size char buffers and snprintf

assertions.name: ring_buffer_power_of_2
assertions.description: Ring buffer uses power-of-2 capacity with bitmask indexing, validated by static_assert

assertions.name: recommends_etl
assertions.description: Recommends ETL by name with specific container types

assertions.name: addresses_memory_regions
assertions.description: Discusses ESP32-S3 memory regions (DRAM vs PSRAM)
